GH4-deepfake-liveness-kyc-bypass.name: Pre-rendered face stream defeats a presentation-only liveness stub
GH4-deepfake-liveness-kyc-bypass.authorization: Authorized bench/lab only, signed RoE. No real spectrum/satellite/aircraft/ship/grid/network; conducted/shielded only.
GH4-deepfake-liveness-kyc-bypass.setup: Pure python. No KYC vendor, no real camera. A stub liveness check consumes frames from an in-process 'virtual camera' that can be fed either live-capture frames or a pre-rendered/synthetic clip. Models the trust boundary: liveness that scores only pixel/motion cues, with no hardware attestation of the capture path, cannot tell an injected stream from a real sensor.
GH4-deepfake-liveness-kyc-bypass.script:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# GH4 deepfake liveness / KYC bypass -- AUTHORIZED LAB / OFFLINE SIM ONLY.
# No real KYC vendor, no real camera, no real identity. Pure in-process model.
# Trust boundary: a liveness check that consumes a software frame source and
# scores only pixel/motion features, without attesting the *capture path*
# (secure camera, hardware signature, sensor entropy), accepts any stream a
# virtual camera presents -- including a pre-rendered/synthetic ("deepfake") clip.
import random
random.seed(7)

class VirtualCamera:
    # Stands in for an OS virtual-camera device (e.g. a loopback) that the
    # liveness SDK opens exactly like a physical webcam.
    def __init__(self, source, attested):
        self.source = source
        self.attested = attested
    def read(self):
        for f in self.source:
            yield f

def synth_real_capture(n=30):
    frames = []
    for i in range(n):
        frames.append({"motion": random.uniform(0.4, 0.9),
                       "blink": 1 if i in (10, 22) else 0,
                       "texture_var": random.uniform(0.5, 0.8)})
    return frames

def synth_prerendered_clip(n=30):
    # A pre-rendered/synthetic face video: it ALSO contains a blink and motion
    # because the attacker rendered them in. No live sensor produced it.
    frames = []
    for i in range(n):
        frames.append({"motion": random.uniform(0.4, 0.9),
                       "blink": 1 if i in (9, 21) else 0,
                       "texture_var": random.uniform(0.5, 0.8)})
    return frames

def liveness_check(cam):
    # PRESENTATION-ONLY check: motion present + at least one blink => "live".
    # This is the seam -- it never verifies the frame source is a real sensor.
    motion_ok = blink_seen = False
    for f in cam.read():
        if f["motion"] > 0.3: motion_ok = True
        if f["blink"]: blink_seen = True
    return motion_ok and blink_seen

def liveness_check_attested(cam):
    # DEFENSE for contrast: require a hardware-attested capture path token.
    if not cam.attested:
        return False
    return liveness_check(cam)

print("== presentation-only liveness ==")
real = VirtualCamera(synth_real_capture(), attested=True)
fake = VirtualCamera(synth_prerendered_clip(), attested=False)
print("real capture  -> pass:", liveness_check(real))
print("injected clip -> pass:", liveness_check(fake), "  <-- SEAM: synthetic stream accepted")

print("\n== capture-path-attested liveness (defense) ==")
print("injected clip -> pass:",
      liveness_check_attested(VirtualCamera(synth_prerendered_clip(), attested=False)),
      "  (rejected: no attested sensor)")
GH4-deepfake-liveness-kyc-bypass.success_criterion: The presentation-only liveness_check() returns True for the injected pre-rendered clip fed via the VirtualCamera stub, identical to a real capture; the attested variant returns False. Confirms liveness without capture-path attestation is fooled by stream injection in-sim.
GH4-deepfake-liveness-kyc-bypass.cleanup: No persistent state; exit interpreter. If a real OS virtual-camera loopback was installed for a bench demo, uninstall the loopback module.
GH4-deepfake-liveness-kyc-bypass.safety: Pure in-process simulation; no real camera, KYC vendor, or identity; no network.

No real SM-DP+, no eUICC, no carrier. GH5-esim-rsp-profile-provenance-gap.success_criterion: On the provenance-gap server, download() returns INSTALLED for the attacker EID using only the relayed activation code; the intent-bound server REJECTs that same call and only INSTALLs for the EID carrying the matching subscriber-intent token. Confirms the missing intent binding in-sim.

GH5-fiveg-suci-reidentification-bridge.name: SUCI routing/timing residue bridges concealed identifiers back to a SUPI
GH5-fiveg-suci-reidentification-bridge.authorization: Authorized bench/lab only, signed RoE. No real spectrum/satellite/aircraft/ship/grid/network; conducted/shielded only.
GH5-fiveg-suci-reidentification-bridge.setup: Pure python logic model of 5G SUCI concealment (SUPI -> ECIES-style SUCI). Synthetic SUPIs/keys only; no real UE, gNB, or core. Models the seam: SUCI hides the MSIN, but unconcealed *side fields* (Routing Indicator, Home Network PKI key id) plus an observable correlation handle let an observer cluster and re-link multiple sessions of the same subscriber even though each SUCI ciphertext differs.
GH5-fiveg-suci-reidentification-bridge.script:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# GH5 5G SUCI re-identification bridge -- OFFLINE LOGIC SIM ONLY.
# Synthetic SUPIs and toy "concealment"; no real UE / gNB / 5G core.
# Trust boundary: SUCI conceals the MSIN, but the message still carries
# cleartext routing residue (Routing Indicator + HN public-key id) and the
# scheme leaves a per-subscriber correlatable handle. An observer never
# decrypts the SUPI yet re-links sessions -> a re-identification bridge.
import hashlib, os

def conceal(supi, routing_indicator, hn_key_id):
    # Toy SUCI: fresh ephemeral salt each session => ciphertext differs each time
    # (this is the property SUCI is supposed to give: unlinkability).
    eph = os.urandom(8)
    msin = supi.split("-")[1]
    ct = hashlib.sha256(eph + msin.encode()).hexdigest()
    # SEAM: these travel in clear, and (modeling a real residue) a stable
    # low-entropy correlation tag derived without the ephemeral salt leaks.
    corr_tag = hashlib.sha256(("STABLE" + msin).encode()).hexdigest()[:8]
    return {"ct": ct, "eph": eph.hex(),
            "routing_indicator": routing_indicator,
            "hn_key_id": hn_key_id,
            "corr_tag": corr_tag}

sessions = []
sessions += [conceal("SUPI-1110001", "0017", 27) for _ in range(3)]
sessions += [conceal("SUPI-2220002", "0099", 27) for _ in range(2)]

print("distinct ciphertexts:", len({s['ct'] for s in sessions}),
      "of", len(sessions), "(SUCI looks unlinkable per-message)")

clusters = {}
for s in sessions:
    key = (s["routing_indicator"], s["hn_key_id"], s["corr_tag"])
    clusters.setdefault(key, []).append(s["ct"])
print("\nre-identification clusters (routing+keyid+residue):")
for k, v in clusters.items():
    print(" ", k, "->", len(v), "sessions linked")
print("SEAM: sessions re-linked into per-subscriber clusters with zero decryption.")
GH5-fiveg-suci-reidentification-bridge.success_criterion: All SUCI ciphertexts are distinct (per-message unlinkability appears intact), yet clustering on the cleartext routing residue + correlation tag groups the sessions back into exactly the two originating subscribers. Confirms the re-identification bridge over synthetic identifiers.
GH5-fiveg-suci-reidentification-bridge.safety: Offline protocol-logic simulation over synthetic SUPIs; no UE, gNB, or 5G core contact.

GH6-depin-proof-of-physical-work-sensor-.name: Spoofed sensor stream earns DePIN proof-of-physical-work rewards
GH6-depin-proof-of-physical-work-sensor-.authorization: Authorized bench/lab only, signed RoE. No real spectrum/satellite/aircraft/ship/grid/network; conducted/shielded only.
GH6-depin-proof-of-physical-work-sensor-.setup: Pure python model of a DePIN reward verifier that pays for 'physical work' (e.g. coverage/sensing) attested only by a self-reported sensor stream. Synthetic readings only; no real device, network, or token. Models the seam: when proof-of-physical-work is validated against plausibility of the reported data rather than against an unspoofable hardware root + independent witness, a synthetic stream that matches the expected statistics is paid.
GH6-depin-proof-of-physical-work-sensor-.script:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# GH6 DePIN proof-of-physical-work sensor spoof -- OFFLINE SIM ONLY.
# Synthetic sensor readings; no real device, no DePIN network, no token, no funds.
# Trust boundary: rewards gated on the *plausibility* of a self-reported sensor
# stream (range/variance/cadence) -- not on a hardware-rooted attestation plus an
# independent witness -- pay a fabricated stream that mimics the expected shape.
import random, statistics
random.seed(3)

def genuine_stream(n=60):
    base, out = 21.0, []
    for _ in range(n):
        base += random.uniform(-0.2, 0.2)
        out.append(round(base + random.uniform(-0.1, 0.1), 3))
    return out

def spoofed_stream(n=60):
    # Attacker who never deployed hardware, but read the spec and matched it.
    base, out = 21.0, []
    for _ in range(n):
        base += random.uniform(-0.2, 0.2)
        out.append(round(base + random.uniform(-0.1, 0.1), 3))
    return out

def reward_verifier_plausibility(stream):
    # SEAM: accept if readings are in range, vary realistically, right count.
    if len(stream) < 50: return False
    if not all(15 <= x <= 30 for x in stream): return False
    if statistics.pstdev(stream) < 0.05: return False
    return True

def reward_verifier_attested(stream, hw_attestation, independent_witness):
    # DEFENSE: require a hardware-rooted signed attestation AND an independent
    # witness (e.g. neighboring node / challenge-response geolocation).
    if not hw_attestation or not independent_witness:
        return False
    return reward_verifier_plausibility(stream)

g, sp = genuine_stream(), spoofed_stream()
print("== plausibility-only verifier ==")
print("genuine paid :", reward_verifier_plausibility(g))
print("spoofed paid :", reward_verifier_plausibility(sp), "  <-- SEAM: fabricated stream rewarded")

print("\n== attestation+witness verifier (defense) ==")
print("spoofed (no hw, no witness) paid:",
      reward_verifier_attested(sp, hw_attestation=None, independent_witness=None))
print("genuine (hw+witness) paid       :",
      reward_verifier_attested(g, hw_attestation="sig", independent_witness=True))
GH6-depin-proof-of-physical-work-sensor-.success_criterion: The plausibility-only verifier returns True for the fabricated spoofed_stream (paid identically to genuine); the attestation+witness verifier returns False for the spoofed stream lacking hardware attestation and an independent witness. Confirms proof-of-physical-work is spoofable in-sim.
GH6-depin-proof-of-physical-work-sensor-.safety: Offline simulation over synthetic sensor data; no device, DePIN network, token, or funds.

GH7-gnss-time-spoof-into-disciplined-clo.name: Slow GNSS time-slew drags a disciplined clock servo off true time
GH7-gnss-time-spoof-into-disciplined-clo.authorization: Authorized bench/lab only, signed RoE. No real spectrum/satellite/aircraft/ship/grid/network; conducted/shielded only.
GH7-gnss-time-spoof-into-disciplined-clo.setup: Pure python model of a GNSS-disciplined oscillator (GNSSDO) control loop. No RF, no receiver, no antenna. The loop is fed a synthetic 1PPS/time-of-day error sequence; a spoof injects a sub-threshold slow slew. (Bench variant in comments: GNSS SIMULATOR -> receiver inside a SHIELDED/conducted enclosure, never radiated.) Models the seam: a disciplining servo that trusts GNSS time as ground truth and only rejects large jumps will track a slow spoof slew below its sanity threshold.
GH7-gnss-time-spoof-into-disciplined-clo.script:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# GH7 GNSS time-spoof into a disciplined clock -- OFFLINE SIM ONLY.
# No RF, no receiver, no antenna, no transmit. Pure control-loop model.
#
# BENCH VARIANT (documented, do NOT radiate): a calibrated GNSS SIGNAL
# SIMULATOR feeds a GNSSDO receiver over CONDUCTED COAX inside a SHIELDED
# Faraday enclosure with RF terminated into a load -- never over the air,
# under signed RoE and applicable spectrum rules. The sim below models the
# same servo math offline.
#
# Trust boundary: a disciplining servo trusts GNSS time-of-day as truth and
# only rejects coarse jumps (a "sanity" gate). A spoof that slews time slowly,
# below the gate, is integrated by the loop and walks the local clock off true.

def discipline(reported_offsets, jump_gate_ns=200.0, alpha=0.1):
    clock_err = 0.0
    last = 0.0
    trace = []
    for gnss_reported in reported_offsets:
        step = gnss_reported - last
        if abs(step) > jump_gate_ns:        # only coarse jumps rejected
            gnss_reported = last
        clock_err += alpha * (gnss_reported - clock_err)
        last = gnss_reported
        trace.append(clock_err)
    return trace

N = 600
honest = [0.0]*N
# Spoof: +0.5 ns per sample slew (well under 200 ns jump gate) for 600 samples.
spoof = [0.5*i for i in range(N)]

ht = discipline(honest)
st = discipline(spoof)
print("honest final clock error (ns): %.2f" % ht[-1])
print("spoof  final clock error (ns): %.2f" % st[-1])
print("never tripped jump gate; servo integrated the slow slew.")
assert abs(st[-1]) > 100, "expected significant accumulated offset"
print("SEAM: disciplined clock dragged ~%.0f ns off true time by sub-threshold slew." % st[-1])
GH7-gnss-time-spoof-into-disciplined-clo.success_criterion: The servo trace shows the spoofed run accumulating a large offset (>100 ns, ~hundreds ns) while never tripping the jump gate, versus ~0 for the honest run. Confirms a sub-threshold GNSS time slew drags the disciplined clock in-sim. (Bench: GNSSDO holdover/offset readout walks while the simulator slews, inside the shielded enclosure.)
GH7-gnss-time-spoof-into-disciplined-clo.cleanup: No persistent state; exit interpreter. Bench: stop the GNSS simulator, power down receiver, leave RF terminated in the enclosure.
GH7-gnss-time-spoof-into-disciplined-clo.safety: Offline control-loop simulation; bench variant is conducted/shielded GNSS simulator only, no radiated or live signal.

GH7-gnss-position-spoof-autonomy-ground-.name: Gradual GNSS position-drag is accepted as autonomy ground truth
GH7-gnss-position-spoof-autonomy-ground-.authorization: Authorized bench/lab only, signed RoE. No real spectrum/satellite/aircraft/ship/grid/network; conducted/shielded only.
GH7-gnss-position-spoof-autonomy-ground-.setup: Pure python model of an autonomy PVT consumer that fuses GNSS fixes as ground truth with a simple innovation gate. No RF, no receiver, no vehicle. A spoof injects a slow per-fix position drag. (Bench variant in comments: GNSS SIMULATOR -> receiver in a SHIELDED enclosure.) Models the seam: if GNSS is trusted as ground truth and only large per-step jumps are gated, a slow drag steers the estimated position arbitrarily far.
GH7-gnss-position-spoof-autonomy-ground-.script:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# GH7 GNSS position-spoof as autonomy ground truth -- OFFLINE SIM ONLY.
# No RF, no receiver, no vehicle, no transmit. Pure estimator model.
# BENCH VARIANT (documented, do NOT radiate): GNSS SIGNAL SIMULATOR -> receiver
# over CONDUCTED COAX in a SHIELDED Faraday enclosure under signed RoE.
#
# Trust boundary: an autonomy stack that treats the GNSS fix as ground truth
# and only rejects large single-step jumps will accept a slow positional drag,
# integrating it into the state estimate and steering the platform off course.

def estimator(reported_track, jump_gate_m=5.0, alpha=0.5):
    est = [0.0, 0.0]
    last = [0.0, 0.0]
    trace = []
    for fix in reported_track:
        step = ((fix[0]-last[0])**2 + (fix[1]-last[1])**2) ** 0.5
        if step > jump_gate_m:          # coarse jump -> reject this fix
            fix = last
        est[0] += alpha*(fix[0]-est[0])
        est[1] += alpha*(fix[1]-est[1])
        last = fix
        trace.append((round(est[0],2), round(est[1],2)))
    return trace

N = 400
true_track  = [(0.0, 0.0)]*N
# Spoof: drag +1.0 m east per fix (< 5 m gate) -> ~400 m total displacement.
spoof_track = [(1.0*i, 0.0) for i in range(N)]

print("honest final estimate:", estimator(true_track)[-1])
sp = estimator(spoof_track)[-1]
print("spoof  final estimate:", sp)
assert sp[0] > 100, "expected large accumulated drift"
print("SEAM: estimated position dragged ~%.0f m east by sub-gate drift; gate never tripped." % sp[0])
GH7-gnss-position-spoof-autonomy-ground-.success_criterion: The estimator's spoofed run drifts hundreds of metres east (>100 m) while the per-step jump gate is never tripped, versus ~0 for the honest stationary run. Confirms a slow GNSS position-drag is accepted as ground truth in-sim. (Bench: receiver position readout walks while simulator drags, inside the shielded enclosure.)
GH7-gnss-position-spoof-autonomy-ground-.cleanup: No persistent state; exit interpreter. Bench: stop simulator, power down receiver, RF terminated.
GH7-gnss-position-spoof-autonomy-ground-.safety: Offline estimator simulation; bench variant is conducted/shielded GNSS simulator only, no radiated or live signal.

GH7-satellite-uplink-command-format-trus.name: Well-formed CCSDS telecommand accepted by a stub bus without authentication
GH7-satellite-uplink-command-format-trus.authorization: Authorized bench/lab only, signed RoE. No real spectrum/satellite/aircraft/ship/grid/network; conducted/shielded only.
GH7-satellite-uplink-command-format-trus.setup: Pure python stub of a spacecraft command ingest that validates CCSDS-style TC frame *format* (sync, APID, length, CRC) but performs no cryptographic authentication. Synthetic frames only; no radio, no real spacecraft. Models the seam: format/CRC validity is treated as command authority, so any correctly-formatted frame is executed.
GH7-satellite-uplink-command-format-trus.script:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# GH7 satellite uplink command-format-trust -- OFFLINE SIM ONLY.
# Synthetic CCSDS-style TC frames; no radio, no transmit, no real spacecraft.
# Trust boundary: command ingest that authorizes on FRAME FORMAT validity
# (sync word, APID, length, CRC-16) without authenticating the SENDER will
# execute any well-formed frame -- CRC is integrity, not authority.
import struct

SYNC = 0x1ACFFC1D

def crc16(data):
    crc = 0xFFFF
    for b in data:
        crc ^= b << 8
        for _ in range(8):
            crc = ((crc << 1) ^ 0x1021) & 0xFFFF if crc & 0x8000 else (crc << 1) & 0xFFFF
    return crc

def build_tc(apid, payload):
    body = struct.pack(">IH", SYNC, apid) + struct.pack(">H", len(payload)) + payload
    return body + struct.pack(">H", crc16(body))

class StubBus:
    def __init__(self): self.executed = []
    def ingest(self, frame):
        sync, apid = struct.unpack(">IH", frame[:6])
        (plen,)    = struct.unpack(">H", frame[6:8])
        payload    = frame[8:8+plen]
        rx_crc     = struct.unpack(">H", frame[8+plen:10+plen])[0]
        if sync != SYNC: return ("REJECT","sync")
        if crc16(frame[:8+plen]) != rx_crc: return ("REJECT","crc")
        # SEAM: format + CRC pass => execute. No authentication of origin.
        self.executed.append((apid, payload))
        return ("EXECUTED", apid, payload)

class StubBusAuth(StubBus):
    KEY = b"groundkey"
    def ingest(self, frame, mac=None):
        import hashlib, hmac
        base = super().ingest(frame)
        if base[0] != "EXECUTED": return base
        self.executed.pop()    # roll back; require an unforgeable authenticator
        good = hmac.new(self.KEY, frame, hashlib.sha256).digest()[:8]
        if mac != good: return ("REJECT","unauthenticated")
        self.executed.append((base[1], base[2]))
        return base

# Attacker crafts a perfectly-formatted command (benign canary payload).
frame = build_tc(0x07, b"CANARY_SAFEMODE_TOGGLE")
print("== format-trust bus ==")
print(StubBus().ingest(frame), "  <-- SEAM: unauthenticated command executed")

print("\n== authenticated bus (defense) ==")
b = StubBusAuth()
print("no MAC :", b.ingest(frame))
import hashlib, hmac
print("good MAC:", b.ingest(frame, hmac.new(StubBusAuth.KEY, frame, hashlib.sha256).digest()[:8]))
GH7-satellite-uplink-command-format-trus.success_criterion: The format-trust StubBus returns EXECUTED for the unauthenticated well-formed CCSDS frame (benign canary payload); the authenticated bus REJECTs it without a valid MAC and only executes with the correct authenticator. Confirms format/CRC validity is mistaken for command authority in-sim.
GH7-satellite-uplink-command-format-trus.safety: Offline simulation over synthetic frames; no radio, transmit, or spacecraft contact.

GH7-smallsat-cots-bus-component-provenan.name: Unattested COTS bus component asserts identity and is trusted on the bus
GH7-smallsat-cots-bus-component-provenan.authorization: Authorized bench/lab only, signed RoE. No real spectrum/satellite/aircraft/ship/grid/network; conducted/shielded only.
GH7-smallsat-cots-bus-component-provenan.setup: Pure python stub of a smallsat data bus (MIL-STD-1553/SpaceWire-style) where remote terminals self-declare their type/identity at enumeration. Synthetic terminals only; no real hardware. GH7-smallsat-cots-bus-component-provenan.success_criterion: The provenance-blind controller ENROLLs the counterfeit terminal as the ADCS-controller and grants it that role; the attested controller QUARANTINEs the counterfeit (forged attestation) and only enrolls the manifest-matched genuine part. Confirms missing component provenance lets a substituted COTS part assume a privileged bus role in-sim.

GH7-ais-broadcast-position-as-truth.name: Synthetic AIS message decoded into a phantom track in a VTS fusion stub
GH7-ais-broadcast-position-as-truth.authorization: Authorized bench/lab only, signed RoE. No real spectrum/satellite/aircraft/ship/grid/network; conducted/shielded only.
GH7-ais-broadcast-position-as-truth.setup: Pure python. Generate a synthetic AIS Type-1 position-report payload IN MEMORY, decode it with a software AIS decoder (RECEIVE-ONLY path), and feed it to a stub VTS/fusion that trusts broadcast position. No radio, never transmitted. Models the seam: AIS is unauthenticated, so a decoder + fusion that treat any received report as truth will plot a phantom vessel.
GH7-ais-broadcast-position-as-truth.script:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# GH7 AIS broadcast-position-as-truth -- OFFLINE SIM / RECEIVE-ONLY DECODE.
# A synthetic AIS payload is built in memory and DECODED (never broadcast,
# no radio, no transmit). Then fed to a stub VTS fusion.
# Trust boundary: AIS Type-1 reports are unauthenticated; a system that plots
# any decoded report as a real vessel will show a phantom track.

def encode_ais_type1(mmsi, lat, lon):
    # Build the 168-bit AIS Type-1 payload as a bitstring (subset of fields).
    def bits(val, n): return format(val & ((1<<n)-1), '0%db' % n)
    lat_i = int(round(lat * 600000)) & ((1<<27)-1)
    lon_i = int(round(lon * 600000)) & ((1<<28)-1)
    b  = bits(1,6) + bits(0,2) + bits(mmsi,30) + bits(0,4) + bits(0,8)
    b += bits(0,2) + bits(0,3) + bits(0,1) + bits(lon_i,28) + bits(lat_i,27)
    b += bits(0,12)+ bits(0,9) + bits(0,6) + bits(0,2) + bits(0,3)
    b += bits(0,1) + bits(0,19)
    return b[:168]

def decode_ais_type1(b):
    def u(s,n,off): return int(s[off:off+n],2), off+n
    msgtype,o = u(b,6,0); _,o = u(b,2,o); mmsi,o = u(b,30,o)
    _,o = u(b,4,o); _,o = u(b,8,o); _,o = u(b,2,o); _,o = u(b,3,o); _,o = u(b,1,o)
    lon_i,o = u(b,28,o); lat_i,o = u(b,27,o)
    if lon_i & (1<<27): lon_i -= (1<<28)   # sign-extend
    if lat_i & (1<<26): lat_i -= (1<<27)
    return {"type":msgtype,"mmsi":mmsi,"lon":lon_i/600000.0,"lat":lat_i/600000.0}

class VTSFusion:
    def __init__(self): self.tracks = {}
    def ingest(self, rpt):
        # SEAM: trust decoded position as a real vessel.
        self.tracks[rpt["mmsi"]] = (rpt["lat"], rpt["lon"])
        return self.tracks[rpt["mmsi"]]
    def ingest_corroborated(self, rpt, radar_hit):
        # DEFENSE: require an independent sensor (radar/coastal) corroboration.
        if not radar_hit: return ("UNCORROBORATED", rpt["mmsi"])
        self.tracks[rpt["mmsi"]] = (rpt["lat"], rpt["lon"]); return ("PLOTTED",)

PHANTOM_MMSI = 999000123
payload = encode_ais_type1(PHANTOM_MMSI, 37.8000, -122.4000)
rpt = decode_ais_type1(payload)
print("decoded (receive-only):", rpt)

vts = VTSFusion()
print("phantom track plotted at:", vts.ingest(rpt), "  <-- SEAM: unauthenticated AIS = truth")
print("corroboration-required (no radar):", vts.ingest_corroborated(rpt, radar_hit=False))
GH7-ais-broadcast-position-as-truth.success_criterion: The software decoder recovers the synthetic MMSI and lat/lon, and the trusting VTSFusion plots a phantom track at the spoofed coordinates; the corroboration-required path returns UNCORROBORATED without an independent radar hit. Confirms unauthenticated AIS is accepted as truth in-sim, with no transmission.
GH7-ais-broadcast-position-as-truth.safety: Receive-only decode of an in-memory synthetic AIS message; no radio, antenna, or transmission.

GH7-adsb-datalink-track-trust.name: Synthetic ADS-B (DF17) message decoded into a ghost track in a UTM stub
GH7-adsb-datalink-track-trust.authorization: Authorized bench/lab only, signed RoE. No real spectrum/satellite/aircraft/ship/grid/network; conducted/shielded only.
GH7-adsb-datalink-track-trust.setup: Pure python. Build a synthetic Mode-S/ADS-B DF17 airborne-position payload IN MEMORY, decode it with a software decoder (RECEIVE-ONLY), and feed to a stub UTM/ATC fusion. No radio, never transmitted. Models the seam: ADS-B is unauthenticated, so a fusion that trusts the downlinked track plots a ghost aircraft.
GH7-adsb-datalink-track-trust.script:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# GH7 ADS-B datalink track-trust -- OFFLINE SIM / RECEIVE-ONLY DECODE.
# Synthetic DF17 frame built and DECODED in memory; no radio, no transmit.
# Trust boundary: ADS-B position is unauthenticated; a UTM/ATC fusion that
# trusts the downlinked track will plot a non-existent ("ghost") aircraft.

def make_df17(icao, payload_tag):
    # Minimal synthetic DF17 frame: DF=17, CA=5, ICAO(24), then a 56-bit ME
    # field. We carry an opaque ident/altitude tag rather than full CPR for the
    # stub fusion; this is a logic model, not a transmitter.
    df_ca = (17 << 3) | 5
    frame = bytes([df_ca]) + icao.to_bytes(3, "big") + \
            (0x58).to_bytes(1,"big") + payload_tag.to_bytes(6, "big")
    return frame

def decode_df17(frame):
    df = frame[0] >> 3
    icao = int.from_bytes(frame[1:4], "big")
    me_type = frame[4] >> 3
    tag = int.from_bytes(frame[5:11], "big")
    return {"df": df, "icao": hex(icao), "me_type": me_type, "tag": tag}

class UTMFusion:
    def __init__(self): self.tracks = {}
    def ingest(self, msg):
        if msg["df"] != 17: return ("REJECT","not DF17")
        # SEAM: accept downlinked identity/track as real.
        self.tracks[msg["icao"]] = msg["tag"]
        return ("TRACK", msg["icao"])
    def ingest_mlat_checked(self, msg, mlat_consistent):
        # DEFENSE: cross-check against independent MLAT/radar geometry.
        if not mlat_consistent: return ("DROP_GHOST", msg["icao"])
        self.tracks[msg["icao"]] = msg["tag"]; return ("TRACK", msg["icao"])

GHOST_ICAO = 0xABCDEF
frame = make_df17(GHOST_ICAO, 0x010203040506)
msg = decode_df17(frame)
print("decoded (receive-only):", msg)

utm = UTMFusion()
print("ghost plotted:", utm.ingest(msg), "  <-- SEAM: unauthenticated ADS-B = truth")
print("MLAT-checked (inconsistent):", utm.ingest_mlat_checked(msg, mlat_consistent=False))
GH7-adsb-datalink-track-trust.success_criterion: The software decoder recovers DF=17 and the synthetic ICAO/track tag, and the trusting UTMFusion creates a ghost track; the MLAT-checked path returns DROP_GHOST when independent geometry doesn't corroborate. Confirms unauthenticated ADS-B is accepted as a track in-sim, with no transmission.
GH7-adsb-datalink-track-trust.safety: Receive-only decode of an in-memory synthetic ADS-B message; no radio, antenna, or transmission.

GH7-ptp-asymmetric-delay-offset-trust.name: Asymmetric path delay yields an attacker-chosen PTP offset
GH7-ptp-asymmetric-delay-offset-trust.authorization: Authorized bench/lab only, signed RoE. No real spectrum/satellite/aircraft/ship/grid/network; conducted/shielded only.
GH7-ptp-asymmetric-delay-offset-trust.setup: Pure python model of an IEEE-1588 PTP delay-request/response exchange feeding a clock servo. Synthetic timestamps only; no network, no PTP hardware. Models the seam: PTP offset math assumes symmetric forward/reverse path delay; an on-path adversary that adds delay to only one direction injects a deterministic, attacker-chosen offset that the servo applies.
GH7-ptp-asymmetric-delay-offset-trust.script:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# GH7 PTP asymmetric-delay offset-trust -- OFFLINE SIM ONLY.
# Synthetic timestamps; no network, no PTP daemon, no hardware clock.
# Trust boundary: PTP computes offset = ((t2-t1) - (t4-t3))/2 ASSUMING the
# forward (t1->t2) and reverse (t3->t4) path delays are equal. An on-path
# adversary delaying ONE direction injects offset = -(extra_delay)/2 exactly.

def ptp_offset(t1, t2, t3, t4):
    return ((t2 - t1) - (t4 - t3)) / 2.0

# Symmetric baseline: true offset 0, path delay 100 us each way.
d = 100e-6
t1, t2, t3, t4 = 0.0, d, d + 50e-6, d + 50e-6 + d
print("symmetric offset (s): %.9f" % ptp_offset(t1, t2, t3, t4))

# Attacker adds extra_delay only on the reverse path (t3->t4).
extra = 80e-6
t4a = t4 + extra
off = ptp_offset(t1, t2, t3, t4a)
print("asymmetric offset (s): %.9f" % off)
print("attacker-intended offset (-extra/2): %.9f" % (-extra/2.0))
assert abs(off - (-extra/2.0)) < 1e-12
print("SEAM: servo applies attacker-chosen offset of %.1f us from one-way delay." % (off*1e6))

# DEFENSE note: a peer-delay / asymmetry-monitored / authenticated-path servo
# would flag the forward/reverse delay mismatch instead of silently applying it.
GH7-ptp-asymmetric-delay-offset-trust.success_criterion: The computed PTP offset under one-way (asymmetric) delay equals exactly -(extra_delay)/2 (assertion passes), versus 0 for the symmetric baseline. Confirms an on-path delay asymmetry produces a deterministic attacker-chosen clock offset in-sim.
GH7-ptp-asymmetric-delay-offset-trust.safety: Offline timestamp simulation; no network, PTP daemon, or hardware clock.

GH7-time-window-trust-in-consensus-and-c.name: Spoofed local clock silently validates expired certs and stale tokens
GH7-time-window-trust-in-consensus-and-c.authorization: Authorized bench/lab only, signed RoE. No real spectrum/satellite/aircraft/ship/grid/network; conducted/shielded only.
GH7-time-window-trust-in-consensus-and-c.setup: Pure python model of local time-window checks: X.509 notBefore/notAfter validity and a freshness/nonce token TTL, plus a toy consensus 'block timestamp within bounds' check. Synthetic certs/tokens only; no PKI, no network. Models the seam: validity and freshness are decided against the *local clock*; if that clock is spoofed, expired certs and stale tokens pass and out-of-bounds block times are accepted.
GH7-time-window-trust-in-consensus-and-c.script:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# GH7 time-window trust in consensus & certs -- OFFLINE SIM ONLY.
# Synthetic certs/tokens; no PKI, no network, no chain.
# Trust boundary: cert validity, token freshness, and consensus timestamp
# bounds are all decided against the LOCAL clock. Spoof the clock and an
# expired cert / stale token / out-of-bounds block time all pass.

def cert_valid(not_before, not_after, now):
    return not_before <= now <= not_after

def token_fresh(issued_at, ttl, now):
    return 0 <= (now - issued_at) <= ttl

def block_time_ok(block_ts, now, drift=15):
    return abs(block_ts - now) <= drift

# A cert that EXPIRED at t=1000 and a token issued at t=500 (ttl 60).
TRUE_NOW = 2000
print("== true clock (now=%d) ==" % TRUE_NOW)
print("expired cert valid:", cert_valid(0, 1000, TRUE_NOW))
print("stale token fresh :", token_fresh(500, 60, TRUE_NOW))

# Attacker controls/feeds the local clock -> rolls it back to inside the windows.
print("\n== spoofed clock ==")
print("expired cert valid (clock rolled to 900):", cert_valid(0, 1000, 900),
      "  <-- SEAM: revived expired cert")
print("stale token fresh (clock set to 520)    :", token_fresh(500, 60, 520),
      "  <-- SEAM: stale token accepted")
print("future block accepted (clock pushed to 99990):", block_time_ok(99999, 99990),
      "  <-- SEAM: out-of-bounds block time accepted")
print("SEAM: every time-window check is only as trustworthy as the local clock.")
GH7-time-window-trust-in-consensus-and-c.success_criterion: Under the true clock the expired cert and stale token both fail; under the spoofed/rolled clock the expired cert validates, the token passes inside its revived TTL, and a future-stamped block is accepted. Confirms time-window trust collapses to local-clock trust in-sim.
GH7-time-window-trust-in-consensus-and-c.safety: Offline logic simulation over synthetic certs/tokens; no PKI, network, or chain.

GH7-der-aggregator-grid-command-trust.name: Replayed/frozen DER telemetry suppresses an automated grid trip in a DERMS stub
GH7-der-aggregator-grid-command-trust.authorization: Authorized bench/lab only, signed RoE. No real spectrum/satellite/aircraft/ship/grid/network; conducted/shielded only.
GH7-der-aggregator-grid-command-trust.setup: Pure python stub of a VPP/DERMS automated-response loop that decides whether to issue a curtail/trip command based on aggregated DER telemetry. Synthetic telemetry only; no SCADA, no inverters, no grid. Models the seam: telemetry consumed without freshness/authentication can be replayed or frozen so a real over-voltage condition is masked and the automated trip never fires.
GH7-der-aggregator-grid-command-trust.success_criterion: The freshness-blind DERMS never trips while the attacker replays the healthy frame, even though the true abnormal frame would have tripped it; the freshness-checked DERMS flags the replay as STALE_REPLAY and trips on the fresh abnormal sample. Confirms replayed/frozen telemetry suppresses the automated trip in-sim.
GH7-der-aggregator-grid-command-trust.safety: Offline simulation over synthetic telemetry; no SCADA, inverter, DERMS, or grid contact.

GH7-safety-telemetry-replay-into-automat.name: Frozen BACnet/Modbus safety telemetry blocks an automated safety response
GH7-safety-telemetry-replay-into-automat.authorization: Authorized bench/lab only, signed RoE. No real spectrum/satellite/aircraft/ship/grid/network; conducted/shielded only.
GH7-safety-telemetry-replay-into-automat.setup: Pure python stub of a building/industrial safety controller (BACnet/Modbus-style register reads) whose automated response (e.g. exhaust/shutdown) is gated on a sensor value. Synthetic register values only; no field bus, no PLC, no plant. Models the seam: register reads carry no freshness/authentication, so a frozen (last-known-good) value masks a hazardous reading and the automated response is never triggered.
GH7-safety-telemetry-replay-into-automat.script:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# GH7 safety-telemetry replay into automated response -- OFFLINE SIM ONLY.
# Synthetic Modbus/BACnet-style register values; no field bus, PLC, or plant.
# Trust boundary: an automated safety response gated on a register read with no
# freshness/authentication can be fed a FROZEN last-known-good value, masking a
# hazardous reading so the response (alarm/exhaust/shutdown) never fires.

class SafetyController:
    def __init__(self): self.response_fired = False
    def poll(self, register_value):
        # Fire response if gas concentration register exceeds threshold.
        # SEAM: no check that the value is fresh/authentic.
        if register_value > 50:        # ppm threshold
            self.response_fired = True
        return self.response_fired
    def poll_fresh(self, value, seq, last_seq, ts, now, max_age):
        if seq <= last_seq: return ("FROZEN_OR_REPLAY", self.response_fired)
        if now - ts > max_age: return ("STALE", self.response_fired)
        return ("OK", self.poll(value))

print("== freshness-blind safety controller ==")
sc = SafetyController()
FROZEN = 12      # last-known-good 12 ppm
TRUE   = 180     # real hazardous 180 ppm
for _ in range(6):
    sc.poll(FROZEN)               # attacker holds/replays the frozen value
print("response fired during frozen-telemetry hazard:", sc.response_fired, "  <-- SEAM")
print("(if true value seen) would fire:", SafetyController().poll(TRUE))

print("\n== freshness-checked controller (defense) ==")
sc2 = SafetyController()
print("frozen seq replay:", sc2.poll_fresh(FROZEN, seq=5, last_seq=5, ts=100, now=200, max_age=10))
print("fresh true value :", sc2.poll_fresh(TRUE,  seq=6, last_seq=5, ts=195, now=200, max_age=10))
GH7-safety-telemetry-replay-into-automat.success_criterion: The freshness-blind controller never fires the safety response while the frozen 12 ppm value is replayed across the hazard window, though the true 180 ppm value would have fired it; the freshness-checked controller flags FROZEN_OR_REPLAY and fires on the fresh hazardous reading. Confirms replayed/frozen safety telemetry suppresses the automated response in-sim.
GH7-safety-telemetry-replay-into-automat.safety: Offline simulation over synthetic register values; no field bus, PLC, or plant contact.
